Looking at the failing tests information, I see that most of the TestDateTimeZoneCutover tests are still failing after mutation. However, the TestMonthDay_Properties test changed from failing to passing because the mutant fixed it.

So when the mutant added these new mappings, maybe one of them indirectly fixed how the month names are retrieved by ensuring the correct locale or timezone is used, making TestMonthDay_Properties pass.

But since all other DateTimeZoneCutover tests are still failing, it indicates that those mutants didn't affect their outcomes. This might be because the changes were in different parts of the codebase (like adding new mappings) and not directly related to how date transitions or cutoffs are handled, which is what TestDateTimeZoneCutover checks.

Therefore, the mutant affected a specific part of the code that fixed one test but didn't interfere with others.
